query: CIA Director John Ratcliffe visit to Cuba May 2026 details and any follow-up visits by US cabinet officials planned
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the findings:

## CIA Director Ratcliffe's May 2026 Cuba Visit

**The visit (May 14, 2026):** CIA Director John Ratcliffe made a rare trip to Havana to meet senior Cuban officials — **Raúl "Raulito" Guillermo Rodríguez Castro** (grandson of Raúl Castro), **Interior Minister Lázaro Álvarez Casas**, and **the head of Cuban intelligence**. He personally delivered Trump's message that the US would engage on economic/security matters "only if Cuba makes fundamental changes," discussing intelligence cooperation, economic stability, and security. The visit coincided with Cuba announcing it had run out of oil and diesel amid the US fuel blockade, and marked the first US government flights landing in Cuba (outside Guantanamo) since 2016. Days later, the US indicted 94-year-old Raúl Castro over the 1996 Brothers to the Rescue shootdown.

## Follow-Up Cabinet Visits

**No follow-up US cabinet-level visit to Cuba was publicly announced or confirmed as "planned"** after Ratcliffe's trip. Instead, the trajectory turned toward escalation:

- **Before Ratcliffe:** A senior State Department delegation visited (~April 10), and Rubio secretly met Raulito in St. Kitts (February 2026).
- **May 22, 2026:** Rubio said he was "doubtful" of diplomacy; the US came away "unimpressed," imposed more sanctions, while Trump renewed military-intervention threats.
- **June 30, 2026 (latest):** Cuban FM Bruno Rodríguez declared talks at a standstill with no progress and announced Cuba would seek a UN General Assembly session on July 7, 2026.

**Bottom line:** The quiet high-level visit track (State Dept → Ratcliffe) gave way to a stalled, deteriorating diplomatic situation with no announced future cabinet visit as of late June 2026.

I note one caveat on sourcing: several key facts rest on single-outlet reporting (CBS, NPR, Wikipedia's crisis timeline). The full structured findings with all sources and data points have been submitted.

sources.title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe makes rare trip to Cuba as island nation faces energy crisis
sources.snippet: Ratcliffe traveled to Havana Thursday, delivered message US prepared to expand engagement if Havana 'makes fundamental changes'; met Raúl Rodriguez Castro, Interior Minister Lazaro Alvarez Casas, head of intelligence.

sources.title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met with Raul Castro's grandson in Havana, US and Cuban officials say
sources.snippet: Ratcliffe met Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro, Interior Minister Lázaro Álvarez Casas, head of Cuban intelligence; first US flights to Cuba outside Guantanamo since 2016; Rubio previously met Rodríguez Castro in St. Kitts in February.

sources.title: Rubio doubtful of diplomacy with Cuba as Trump raises new threat of military action
sources.snippet: Rubio says US prefers negotiated agreement but doubtful; top aides including Rubio and Ratcliffe met Cuban officials but came away unimpressed, leading to more sanctions; Castro indicted; Trump renews military threat.

sources.title: Cuba's foreign minister observes 'no progress' in US negotiations
sources.snippet: Cuban FM Bruno Rodríguez describes negotiations with the US as at a standstill, accuses US of constant threats, announces UN debate.

sources.title: Bruno Rodríguez Criticizes Stalled U.S.-Cuba Talks, Calls for U.N. Session
sources.snippet: Rodríguez declared talks at a standstill with no signs of progress; Cuba plans to request a UN General Assembly session on July 7 to address US sanctions.

sources.title: 2026 Cuban crisis
sources.snippet: US fuel blockade began February 2026; on 14 May 2026 Cuba's Ministry of Energy and Mines warned the country had run out of oil and diesel.

sources.title: CIA director visits Cuba for rare meeting as island runs out of fuel
sources.snippet: Reported Ratcliffe's May 14 visit and talks with Raulito (access blocked; headline/date only).

sources.title: Cuba: High-level US delegation visits as Trump ups pressure
sources.snippet: A senior US delegation met Cuban officials in one of the highest-level US visits in roughly a decade (April 2026).

key_facts: CIA Director John Ratcliffe visited Havana, Cuba on Thursday, May 14, 2026 for a rare high-level meeting with senior Cuban officials.

He met Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro ('Raulito,' grandson of Raúl Castro and former head of Cuba's Secret Service equivalent), Interior Minister Lázaro Álvarez Casas, and the head of Cuban intelligence services.

Ratcliffe delivered Trump's message that the US would engage on economic/security issues 'only if Cuba makes fundamental changes,' discussing intelligence cooperation, economic stability, and security.
The visit coincided with Cuba's Energy Ministry warning on May 14, 2026 that the island had run out of oil and diesel amid a US fuel blockade and grid collapse.
These US-Cuba meetings mark the first US government flights landing in Cuba outside Guantanamo Bay since 2016.
Days after the visit (May 20-21, 2026), the US indicted 94-year-old Raúl Castro over the 1996 Brothers to the Rescue shootdown (murder, destruction of an airplane).
Prior high-level contacts: a senior State Department delegation visited Cuba (~April 10, 2026) and Secretary of State Marco Rubio secretly met Raulito in St. Kitts (February 2026).
No new or additional US cabinet-level visit to Cuba was publicly announced as 'planned' after Ratcliffe's May 14 trip.
On May 22, 2026, Rubio said he was 'doubtful' of a diplomatic resolution; the US side came away 'unimpressed' and imposed more sanctions while Trump renewed military-intervention threats.
By June 30, 2026, Cuban FM Bruno Rodríguez said talks were at a standstill with no progress and announced Cuba would seek a UN General Assembly session on July 7, 2026 on US sanctions.
Ratcliffe had made a parallel Cabinet-level trip to Caracas, Venezuela in January 2026 after the US operation removing Nicolás Maduro.

data_points.metric: Date of CIA Director Ratcliffe's Cuba visit
US humanitarian aid offered to Cuba (conditional)
Year of last prior US government flights landing in Cuba (outside Guantanamo) before 2026 talks
Raúl Castro age at time of US indictment
Planned UN General Assembly session date on US sanctions (requested by Cuba)
Number of political prisoners Cuba initially agreed to release amid talks
